security.csp: default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; font-src 'self' data:; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' ipc: http://ipc.localhost; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'

bundle._targetsNote: AppImage is omitted: its linuxdeploy/gtk bundling is flaky on CI and headless boxes, and it aborts the whole Linux job (losing the good .deb). Ship .deb for Linux; revisit AppImage later. Tauri builds only the targets applicable per OS (deb=Linux, app+dmg=macOS, nsis+msi=Windows).
bundle.shortDescription: Run a Circuit node — GPU inference or CPU agent hosting.
bundle.longDescription: Circuit Node turns your computer into part of the Circuit network. Lend an NVIDIA GPU to the decentralized inference mesh, or lend spare CPU to the agent cloud that hosts other people's agents — set a budget, click once, and earn. Wraps the same node-client the command-line install uses.
